seoTitle: Mount George vs Mount Jagged — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount George and Mount Jagged. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount George vs Mount Jagged: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount George and Mount Jagged.
heading: Mount George vs Mount Jagged — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount George, South Australia and Mount Jagged, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount George has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Mount Jagged (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount George or Mount Jagged safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount George has a flood risk score of 20/100, while Mount Jagged scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount George scores 49/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Jagged scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount George or Mount Jagged?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount George has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
